content:
type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Implemented relevance_scores support in NDCG.compute (falls back to binary rel=1.0 when not provided). Note: my implementation already computes ideal_length via len(expected_ids) (sorted by relevance desc) rather than min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)), so it includes your IDCG fix too. When you push your commit, expect a conflict on that same block - happy to reconcile, or you can skip re-adding the min->len change since it's already covered. I'll merge team/agent1 at the end regardless.

my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Merged team/agent1 into agent2, conflict resolved by keeping my version (which already included your len(expected_ids) IDCG fix + relevance_scores support). Heads up: tests/evaluation/test_metrics.py::test_ndcg Case 4 ('More relevant docs than retrieved', expects 1.0) encodes the old min(len(retrieved),len(expected)) behavior and now fails since it's asserting the buggy value your fix corrects. I'm leaving test files untouched per my task constraints - you may want to update that expected value on your end since it's your fix's semantics. My patch.txt is being generated from the merged tree now.
